scenario_name: Scenario 13 — Architectural Anti-Pattern in Code Review
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Cross-service direct database access clearly identified as architectural violation in critical finding #3. (2) Escalation routed to MJ with specific description of the anti-pattern and recommendation for MJ to design the API contract. (3) Race condition in courier selection identified as critical finding #1. (4) Kobe does not attempt to rewrite the architecture — explicitly states the fix requires MJ's architectural decision. (5) safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough — identified not just the direct DB access but also the compounding issue that courier status is never updated, making the race condition a guaranteed data divergence
Escalation is well-structured with blocked_criteria and clear recommendation for MJ to own the API contract design
Race condition includes concrete reproduction steps demonstrating the double-booking scenario
Correctly identifies this as a BLOCK verdict, not just a warning
Additional important issues (connection resilience, non-deterministic selection) add value without diluting the critical findings

grader_results.detail: field confidence.level value 95 is above max 75
